title: containers.conf.5: no documentation for runtimes table
body:
containers.conf.5.md contains under table engine:

> runtime=""
> 
> Default OCI specific runtime in runtimes that will be used by default. Must refer to a member of the runtimes table. Default runtime will be searched for on the system using the priority: "crun", "runc", "runj", "kata", "runsc", "ocijail"

* In the first sentence I do not understand what means “in runtimes”.  How does "in runtimes" change the meaning, compared to when it is missing?

* For me „Default runtime … that will be used by default” sounds like tautology.

* In the second sentence “of the runtimes table” does mean “of the engines.runtimes” table, which gets evident only after looking in pkg/config/containers.conf.

* There is no documentation for the engines.runtimes table.

comments.body:
If `/etc/containers/containers.conf` contains:
```
[engine]
runtime="crun"
```
and has no `engines.runtimes` table, `podman-5.5.3-dev info` shows under ociRuntime.name "crun" and `podman run` uses `crun`.  So “Must refer to a member of the runtimes table. ” seems incorrect.
